LanguageData.VEE_AnimaPearls.description: A smooth, lustrous pearl formed within anima blossoms, faintly humming with psychic resonance. Anima pearls can be consumed to increase psychic sensitivity for some time.
LanguageData.VEE_AnimaPearls.label: anima pearl
LanguageData.VEE_ButtercupFlower.label: buttercup
LanguageData.VEE_Cenote.description: A deep, water filled sinkhole carved into the stone. The surface appears calm. Due to its depth, it can't be filled.
LanguageData.VEE_Cenote.label: cenote
LanguageData.VEE_Cenote_Small.description: A deep, water filled sinkhole carved into the stone. The surface appears calm. Due to its depth, it can't be filled.
LanguageData.VEE_Cenote_Small.label: small cenote
LanguageData.VEE_Coral.description: A colorful, calcified colony of tiny marine organisms, forming intricate reef structures teeming with life.
LanguageData.VEE_Coral.label: coral
LanguageData.VEE_DayBoomshroom.description: These mushrooms produce volatile chemicals as a defensive measure. They can be harvested for chemfuel. However, this must be done with extreme caution, as the mushrooms can explode if they're destroyed.\n\nGlitterworld researchers have used genetic material from boomshrooms to bioengineer a number of chemfuel-producing animals.
LanguageData.VEE_DayBoomshroom.label: light-resistant boomshroom
LanguageData.VEE_DayPsilocap.description: A fungus that gives off hallucinogenic spores. Standing next to them is enough to produce mild hallucinations. Psilocaps can be harvested and ingested to produce a vivid sensory experience that increases the user's mood.
LanguageData.VEE_DayPsilocap.label: light-resistant psilocap
LanguageData.VEE_DayWillowgill.description: A tall spindly slow-growing mushroom with gills that droop like a willow tree.
LanguageData.VEE_DayWillowgill.label: light-resistant willowgill
LanguageData.VEE_DeadlifeVent.description: A sinister geyser that exhales eerie, ash-like dust, capable of reawakening the dead under its influence.
LanguageData.VEE_DeadlifeVent.label: deadlife geyser
LanguageData.VEE_DenseSnow.description: A heavy, compacted layer of snow that resists movement and muffles sound. Can be mined to remove it.
LanguageData.VEE_DenseSnow.label: solid snow
LanguageData.VEE_DriftwoodChunk.description: A sun-bleached fragment of wood, smoothed by waves and time, carried ashore from some distant and unknown origin.
LanguageData.VEE_DriftwoodChunk.label: driftwood
LanguageData.VEE_DryWheat.description: An amber cereal plant that takes almost an entire growing season to come to fruition. This wheat has been subjected to drought conditions for too long and has wilted away.
LanguageData.VEE_DryWheat.label: dry wheat
LanguageData.VEE_Filth_Seashells.label: seashells
LanguageData.VEE_ForgetMeNot.label: forget-me-not
LanguageData.VEE_Gorse.description: A spiny, evergreen shrub covered in bright yellow flowers. Its thick, tangled branches provide shelter while resisting wind and harsh conditions.
LanguageData.VEE_Gorse.label: gorse
LanguageData.VEE_Heather.description: A low, flowering shrub with dense clusters of tiny blooms. It carpets the landscape with muted colors and weathers wind and exposure with ease.
LanguageData.VEE_Heather.label: heather
LanguageData.VEE_InertPitGate.description: A massive, foreboding hole that connects the surface with a dark network of underground caves. It is possible to climb down into the caverns below.
LanguageData.VEE_InertPitGate.label: pit gate
LanguageData.VEE_JadeChunk.description: A chunk of jadeite. It can be deconstructed to acquire some jade.
LanguageData.VEE_JadeChunk.label: jadeite chunk
LanguageData.VEE_Knapweed.label: knapweed
LanguageData.VEE_Loosestrife.description: A beautiful herbaceous perennial wild plant.
LanguageData.VEE_Loosestrife.label: loosestrife
LanguageData.VEE_Mangrove.description: A salt-tolerant tree adapted to life in brackish wetlands. Its tangled roots anchor it in shifting mud and provide shelter for aquatic life.
LanguageData.VEE_Mangrove.label: mangrove
LanguageData.VEE_Mangrove_ChoppedStump.description: A stump left behind after a mangrove tree has been felled. The stump can be extracted but yields very little usable wood. Left outdoors, the stump will deteriorate in time.
LanguageData.VEE_Mangrove_ChoppedStump.label: mangrove chopped stump
LanguageData.VEE_Mangrove_SmashedStump.description: The remnants of a mangrove tree destroyed by damage. It's ugly. The stump can be extracted but yields very little usable wood. Left outdoors, the stump will deteriorate in time.
LanguageData.VEE_Mangrove_SmashedStump.label: mangrove smashed stump
LanguageData.VEE_MineableComponentSpacer.description: Advanced ancient electronics, compacted over time. Can be mined for useful advanced components.
LanguageData.VEE_MineableComponentSpacer.label: advanced compacted machinery
LanguageData.VEE_MineableJadeite.description: A silicate mineral in the pyroxene family that is one of the two forms of jade.
LanguageData.VEE_MineableJadeite.label: jadeite
LanguageData.VEE_ObsidianChunk.description: A chunk of obsidian. It can be deconstructed to acquire some obsidian.
LanguageData.VEE_ObsidianChunk.label: obsidian chunk
LanguageData.VEE_Plant_AnimaBell.description: Clumps of vines that burst through the soil to bloom into beautiful flowers. Believed to be part of great collective roots that spread deep under the terrain which occasionally breach the surface to pollinate. The attractive qualities of the flowers encourage humans not to remove them.
LanguageData.VEE_Plant_AnimaBell.label: anima bell
LanguageData.VEE_Plant_AnimaBrambles.description: Thorny, fast-growing vines infused with faint anima energy. Their barbs are humming softly to the touch.
LanguageData.VEE_Plant_AnimaBrambles.label: anima brambles
LanguageData.VEE_Plant_AnimaBush.description: A short shrub. Does talk, but only when you're not around.
LanguageData.VEE_Plant_AnimaBush.label: anima bush
LanguageData.VEE_Plant_AnimaHeartBlossom.description: A delicate, softly glowing flower that blooms near anima trees, resonating faintly with psychic energy. Can be harvested to acquire anima pearls.
LanguageData.VEE_Plant_AnimaHeartBlossom.label: anima heart blossom
LanguageData.VEE_Plant_AnimaHusk.description: A withered anima tree whose bark has hardened into a gray, lifeless shell under relentless pollution. Its psychic presence is muted and distorted, yet a stubborn echo of anima still clings to the husk, refusing to fully fade.
LanguageData.VEE_Plant_AnimaHusk.label: anima husk
LanguageData.VEE_Plant_AnimaRoot.description: Pale, twisting roots shot through with a soft inner glow, drawn from anima-touched soil. Can be harvested for wood.
LanguageData.VEE_Plant_AnimaRoot.label: anima root
LanguageData.VEE_Plant_BarrelCactus.description: A large cactus native to arid Earth environments. Though it is not a tree, it yields a small amount of fibrous material that can be used like wood.
LanguageData.VEE_Plant_BarrelCactus.label: barrel cactus
LanguageData.VEE_Plant_BeavertailCactus.description: A large cactus native to arid Earth environments. Though it is not a tree, it yields a small amount of fibrous material that can be used like wood.
LanguageData.VEE_Plant_BeavertailCactus.label: beavertail cactus
LanguageData.VEE_Plant_ChollaCactus.description: A large cactus native to arid Earth environments. Though it is not a tree, it yields a small amount of fibrous material that can be used like wood.
LanguageData.VEE_Plant_ChollaCactus.label: cholla cactus
LanguageData.VEE_Plant_DatePalm.description: A tall palm thriving along warm coasts, bearing clusters of sweet, energy-rich fruit. Its deep roots and flexible trunk help it withstand salt spray and coastal winds.
LanguageData.VEE_Plant_DatePalm.label: date palm
LanguageData.VEE_Plant_FakeAnimaTree.description: An anima-touched tree. It lacks the psychic potency of a true anima tree, but still hums faintly with psychic life.
LanguageData.VEE_Plant_FakeAnimaTree.label: anima tree
LanguageData.VEE_Plant_Fern.description: A shade-loving plant with broad fronds that spreads across damp forest floors and sheltered ground.
LanguageData.VEE_Plant_Fern.label: fern
LanguageData.VEE_Plant_Firewood.description: A hardy tree adapted to grow on volcanic ground. It is among the first plants to colonize cooled lava, helping life return to the black stone.
LanguageData.VEE_Plant_Firewood.label: firewood tree
LanguageData.VEE_Plant_Firewood_ChoppedStump.descript: A stump left behind after a firewood tree has been felled. The stump can be extracted but yields very little usable wood. Left outdoors, the stump will deteriorate in time.
LanguageData.VEE_Plant_Firewood_ChoppedStump.label: chopped stump
LanguageData.VEE_Plant_Firewood_SmashedStump.descript: The remnants of a firewood tree destroyed by damage. It's ugly. The stump can be extracted but yields very little usable wood. Left outdoors, the stump will deteriorate in time.
LanguageData.VEE_Plant_Firewood_SmashedStump.label: smashed stump
LanguageData.VEE_Plant_GnarledPine.description: A twisted, weathered pine shaped by wind and poor soil. Its gnarled branches and sparse needles give it a rugged, enduring appearance.
LanguageData.VEE_Plant_GnarledPine.label: gnarled pine tree
LanguageData.VEE_Plant_GrandAnimaTree.description: A grand anima-touched tree. It lacks the psychic potency of a true anima tree, but still hums faintly with psychic life.
LanguageData.VEE_Plant_GrandAnimaTree.label: grand anima tree
LanguageData.VEE_Plant_HedgehogCactus.description: A large cactus native to arid Earth environments. Though it is not a tree, it yields a small amount of fibrous material that can be used like wood.
LanguageData.VEE_Plant_HedgehogCactus.label: hedgehog cactus
LanguageData.VEE_Plant_HoodiaCactus.description: A large cactus native to arid Earth environments. Though it is not a tree, it yields a small amount of fibrous material that can be used like wood.
LanguageData.VEE_Plant_HoodiaCactus.label: hoodia cactus
LanguageData.VEE_Plant_JuniperBush.description: A hardy, low-growing bush with aromatic needles and dense berries. Its branches twist and spread, enduring harsh winds and exposed conditions.
LanguageData.VEE_Plant_JuniperBush.label: juniper bush
LanguageData.VEE_Plant_Laurel.description: An evergreen broadleaf tree with dense foliage and aromatic leaves, well adapted to mild, humid climates.
LanguageData.VEE_Plant_Laurel.label: laurel tree
LanguageData.VEE_Plant_LaurelTree_ChoppedStump.descri: A stump left behind after a laurel tree has been felled. The stump can be extracted but yields very little usable wood. Left outdoors, the stump will deteriorate in time.
LanguageData.VEE_Plant_LaurelTree_ChoppedStump.label: chopped stump
LanguageData.VEE_Plant_LaurelTree_SmashedStump.descri: The remnants of a laurel tree destroyed by damage. It's ugly. The stump can be extracted but yields very little usable wood. Left outdoors, the stump will deteriorate in time.
LanguageData.VEE_Plant_LaurelTree_SmashedStump.label: smashed stump
LanguageData.VEE_Plant_MysticCap.description: A small, vividly colored mushroom with a faintly iridescent cap. Can be harvested for raw fungus.
LanguageData.VEE_Plant_MysticCap.label: mystic cap
LanguageData.VEE_Plant_OrganPipeCactus.description: A large cactus native to arid Earth environments. Though it is not a tree, it yields a small amount of fibrous material that can be used like wood.
LanguageData.VEE_Plant_OrganPipeCactus.label: organ pipe cactus
LanguageData.VEE_Plant_ScrewPine.description: A hardy coastal tree with spiraled leaves and exposed roots, adapted to wind, salt, and shifting sand.
LanguageData.VEE_Plant_ScrewPine.label: screw pine
LanguageData.VEE_Plant_ScrewPine_ChoppedStump.descrip: A stump left behind after a screw pine has been felled. The stump can be extracted but yields very little usable wood. Left outdoors, the stump will deteriorate in time.
LanguageData.VEE_Plant_ScrewPine_ChoppedStump.label: chopped stump
LanguageData.VEE_Plant_ScrewPine_SmashedStump.descrip: The remnants of a screw pine destroyed by damage. It's ugly. The stump can be extracted but yields very little usable wood. Left outdoors, the stump will deteriorate in time.
LanguageData.VEE_Plant_ScrewPine_SmashedStump.label: smashed stump
LanguageData.VEE_Plant_TreeAnima_Ancient.description:
A huge rare tree with warm, skin-smooth bark and long iridescent leaves. Infused with bioluminescent micro-organisms, ancient anima trees develop a unique form of psychic symbiosis with surrounding lifeforms.

If a person (psycaster or not) meditates near an ancient anima tree, it will grow anima grass around its base. Once enough grass is grown, it becomes possible to carry out a psychic linking ritual with the tree and upgrade a person's psychic powers. Only tribal peoples know the secret of this ritual. Tribal psycasters are also able to draw psyfocus from ancient anima trees while meditating to them.

Ancient anima trees' psychic properties are weakened if artificial structures are placed nearby. They refuse to be caged or studied, and must remain part of nature.

Most tribes believe that ancient anima trees are not simply trees, but are rather the physical extremities of a single world spirit.
LanguageData.VEE_Plant_TreeAnima_Ancient.label: ancient anima tree
LanguageData.VEE_Plant_TreeBirch_Auburn.description: A temperate-biome tree known for its thin, white, paper-like bark.
LanguageData.VEE_Plant_TreeBirch_Auburn.label: auburn birch tree
LanguageData.VEE_Plant_TreeMaple_Auburn.description: A common medium-sized tree, easily identifiable by its distinct three-pointed leaf.
LanguageData.VEE_Plant_TreeMaple_Auburn.label: auburn maple tree
LanguageData.VEE_Plant_TreeOak_Auburn.description: A hardwood tree. Oaks take a long time to grow, but their wood is so strong that 'oak' is used as a metaphor for strength across many cultures.
LanguageData.VEE_Plant_TreeOak_Auburn.label: auburn oak tree
LanguageData.VEE_Plant_TreePoplar_Auburn.description: A softwood tree that grows very fast. Unfortunately, its wood is weak and so it yields less useful material than hardwood trees.
LanguageData.VEE_Plant_TreePoplar_Auburn.label: auburn poplar tree
LanguageData.VEE_Plant_YoungAnimaTree.description: A small anima-touched tree. It lacks the psychic potency of a true anima tree, but still hums faintly with psychic life.
LanguageData.VEE_Plant_YoungAnimaTree.label: anima sapling
LanguageData.VEE_RadioactiveBarrel.description: A rusted barrel leaking discarded nuclear fuel.
LanguageData.VEE_RadioactiveBarrel.label: radioactive barrel
LanguageData.VEE_RawDates.description: Raw dates. Nice to eat, even when raw. They are sweet, nutrient-rich fruit harvested from date palms. They keep well in warm climates and provide a reliable source of energy.
LanguageData.VEE_RawDates.label: dates
LanguageData.VEE_RotstinkVent.description: A foul geyser that intermittently belches clouds of corpse-born miasma, choking the air with the stench of decay.
LanguageData.VEE_RotstinkVent.label: rotstink geyser
LanguageData.VEE_Sinkhole.description: A sudden collapse in the ground forming a deep, unstable pit, often exposing bare rock beneath the surface.
LanguageData.VEE_Sinkhole.label: sinkhole
LanguageData.VEE_SmokeVent.description: A smoldering fissure that intermittently exhales thick, acrid smoke, reducing visibility and choking the lungs.
LanguageData.VEE_SmokeVent.label: smoke geyser
LanguageData.VEE_SmoothedJadeite.description: A silicate mineral in the pyroxene family that is one of the two forms of jade.
LanguageData.VEE_SmoothedJadeite.label: smoothed jadeite
LanguageData.VEE_SulfurVent.description: A naturally-occurring break in the ground from which sulfuric gases escape. The gas produced on these fissures stinks of rotten eggs.
LanguageData.VEE_SulfurVent.label: sulfur fissure
LanguageData.VEE_ToxicVent.description: A volatile fissure that periodically spews noxious green gas, poisoning the air and sickening anything that breathes it.
LanguageData.VEE_ToxicVent.label: toxic geyser
